description: Open-source .NET ETL library for building data integration pipelines with Data Flow and Control Flow components.
rules: Build a data flow in three steps: instantiate components, connect them with LinkTo(), then call source.ExecuteAsync() and await destination.WaitAsync()
LinkTo() returns a link source so you can chain calls; pass a Predicate<T> to filter/route rows to specific destinations
Data flow runs asynchronously on TPL Dataflow blocks; each component has its own buffer so only the current row (plus buffer) is held in memory
Use CustomSource<T> / CustomDestination<T> to integrate any system not supported out of the box
Non-generic component variants (e.g. Multicast, CustomSource) operate on ExpandoObject/dynamic
Transformations are non-blocking (RowTransformation, Aggregation, MergeJoin, Multicast), partially blocking (Lookup, CrossJoin), or blocking (BlockTransformation, Sort) - choose based on memory needs
Use ControlFlow tasks for one-line DDL/DML: CreateTableTask.Create, SqlTask.ExecuteNonQuery, RowCountTask.Count, etc.
Connect via IConnectionManager implementations (SqlConnectionManager, MySqlConnectionManager, PostgresConnectionManager, SQLiteConnectionManager) - never raw ADO.NET

Logging uses Microsoft.Extensions.Logging; inject an ILogger<T> via the component constructor to log from any data flow or control flow component, and plug in any standard logging provider (Console, Serilog, etc.)
To persist ETL log events into a database table, add the optional EtlKit.Logging.Database package and call DatabaseLoggingConfiguration.AddDatabaseLoggingConfiguration(connectionManager)

EtlKit.Serialization provides XML pipeline configuration via DataFlowXmlReader; use component C# class names as XML element names and C# property names as child element names inside an IDataFlow wrapper class (e.g., EtlDataFlowStep)

In XML: use <Pipeline> to sequence steps — first child may be a source, remaining children are transformations; add a <LinkTo> child inside <Pipeline> to route output to an external destination; steps inside <Pipeline> must not contain their own <LinkTo>
EtlKit.Kafka package: use KafkaJsonSource<T> for JSON Kafka messages (XML element: <KafkaJsonSource>); properties are <Topic> and <ConsumerConfig> (with <BootstrapServers> and <GroupId>)

EtlKit.AI package: use AIBatchTransformation (XML element: <AIBatchTransformation>); properties are <ApiSettings> (with <ApiKey>, <ApiModel>, <ApiBaseUrl>) and <Prompt> (DotLiquid template; batch available as 'input')
EtlKit.Rest package: use RestTransformation (XML element: <RestTransformation>); property is <RestMethodInfo> (with <Url>, <Method>, <Body>, <Headers>)
